title: Operation Shadow Recon
learning_objectives: Execute a full-scale passive intelligence engagement
Synthesise multi-source OSINT into actionable intelligence
key_topics: Personnel and social engineering surface mapping
scenario: A medium-sized financial services company in Southern Africa suspects they are being targeted. Before committing to a full engagement, they want a comprehensive passive intelligence report. Using only OSINT techniques, build a complete picture of their external attack surface, personnel exposure, and technology stack. No active scanning is permitted.

scenario_context.setting: Financial services company in Southern Africa under suspected targeting
scenario_context.challenge: Complete passive intelligence engagement producing executive-ready output
scenario_context.relevance: Financial services are the most targeted sector in Africa. OSINT-only engagements are increasingly common as a first assessment phase.

subtasks.title: Scope Definition
subtasks.description: Define your OSINT scope: target domains, subsidiary companies, key personnel roles to investigate. Document your approach and methodology.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Research the company publicly. Identify all domains, subsidiaries, and related entities. Define what you'll investigate and how. Create a methodology document before starting collection.

subtasks.title: Domain & Infrastructure Intelligence
subtasks.description: Perform full WHOIS, DNS, subdomain enumeration, and technology fingerprinting across all target domains.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Apply everything from OB-M03 at scale. WHOIS all domains. Full DNS enumeration. Subdomain discovery via certificate transparency. Technology fingerprinting. Map all infrastructure.
subtasks.evidence_required: Infrastructure intelligence report with all findings

subtasks.title: Personnel & Social Engineering Surface
subtasks.description: Map organisational structure, identify key personnel, discover email patterns, check breach databases.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: LinkedIn enumeration. Email pattern discovery. Breach database checks. Identify high-value targets (executives, IT admins, finance staff). Assess social engineering risk.

subtasks.title: Technology Stack & Vulnerability Landscape
subtasks.description: Identify visible technologies and cross-reference with known CVEs. Assess the theoretical attack surface.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Map all visible technologies. Cross-reference each with CVE databases. Assess: what could an attacker exploit without active scanning? What's the theoretical attack surface?

subtasks.title: Executive OSINT Report
subtasks.description: Compile a professional executive-ready report with findings, risk ratings, and recommendations.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Professional report: Executive Summary, Methodology, Infrastructure Findings, Personnel Exposure, Technology Landscape, Risk Assessment, Recommendations. Client-ready quality.

reflection_prompt: Your OSINT reveals that the financial company's CEO uses the same password (found in a breach) across multiple personal and professional accounts. This information is clearly in scope. How do you handle this finding in your report?

quiz.question: Full OSINT engagements are valuable to clients because:
options: They reveal what any attacker can learn without triggering any defences
quiz.explanation: OSINT engagements show clients their external exposure from an attacker's perspective, without touching their systems or triggering alerts.

quiz.question: Personnel exposure mapping helps identify:
options: Social engineering targets and organisational knowledge that attackers can exploit
quiz.explanation: Personnel mapping reveals who attackers might target for social engineering, what roles give access to valuable systems, and what personal information is exposed.

mentor_notes: This is the capstone — evaluate as a professional deliverable. The report should be client-ready. Finding completeness and analysis depth are the primary differentiators. Methodology documentation shows professional maturity.
